claims.statement: Worksheet-scaling runner schema v2 and WorkbookEditor benchmark schema v20 recorded balanced fixed-shape 1/2/4-sheet Patch upsert profiles on clean revision e85bbd2b, with 6 warm-up and 12 measured rounds per mixed-inline and formula workload.

On this machine, four fixed-shape mixed-inline worksheets carried four times the source cells and edits of one worksheet with paired total wall/process CPU ratios of 3.970x/3.898x and a paired peak-working-set ratio of 1.0369; the four-sheet median was 3327 ms and 9.36328 MiB.

On this machine, four fixed-shape formula worksheets carried four times the source cells and edits of one worksheet with paired total wall/process CPU ratios of 3.803x/4.074x and a paired peak-working-set ratio of 1.0412; the four-sheet median was 2101 ms and 8.384765 MiB.

All six representative outputs opened with openpyxl 3.1.2 and passed sheet-name, edited-value and dimension checks. Position distributions remained noisy, Office and a second machine were not run, and no fixed-total, arbitrary-workbook, concurrency, direct-zlib, public-backend or full-feature claim is made.
